visual_notes: Obsidian is pure volcanic glass — the most visually simple and the most dramatically effective black mineral material. Its jet-black vitreous surface reflects light cleanly. Rainbow varieties produce full spectral color from thin-film magnetite layers. Apache tears appear opaque but transmit brownish light when held up.
prompt_fragments: jet-black volcanic glass with conchoidal fracture — razor edge from deep time
rainbow obsidian — full spectral iridescence on absolute black surface
Aztec smoking mirror: Tezcatlipoca sees all in volcanic glass
vibe_notes: Obsidian is the void made solid — a black mirror that reflects what you put in front of it. It is simultaneously the sharpest natural material and the darkest. Tezcatlipoca's smoking mirror sees all. Apache tears carry grief transformed into translucent darkness.
folklore_summary: Tezcatlipoca, the Aztec god of night, sorcery, and the north, is symbolized by a black obsidian smoking mirror — his attribute and the means by which he could see all things in the universe including human sin and fate.
